sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: 1. FC Union Berlin have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, displaying defensive vulnerabilities with no clean sheet in their last 3 games. SC Freiburg, on the other hand, have had a slightly better run with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, scoring consistently in recent matches. Freiburg's recent away performances indicate a capability to exploit Union's defensive lapses.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Union Berlin have the upper hand with a 2-1 victory away at Freiburg and a 0-0 draw at home. Historically, Union have not lost to Freiburg in their last 5 meetings, which could provide a psychological boost for the home side despite Freiburg's recent form.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Union Berlin, sitting 10th with 10 points, have struggled defensively, conceding 15 goals (12th in the league), while their attack has managed 11 goals. SC Freiburg, just below at 11th with 9 points, have a similar goal tally (11 scored, 13 conceded) but show better attacking intent in recent games. Union's low possession stats (32.9%, last in the league) could be a concern against Freiburg's more balanced approach.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Union Berlin, Danilho Doekhi has been in fine form, scoring 3 goals in the last 5 matches with a high Sofascore rating of 7.68. Freiburg's Vincenzo Grifo, with 3 goals and a 7.5 rating, poses a significant threat in attack. These players could be decisive in today's outcome.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Despite Union's historical edge in head-to-heads, Freiburg appear to have the momentum with better recent form and attacking output. Sofascore's prediction of a 46% chance for an away win aligns with Freiburg's potential to capitalize on Union's defensive issues. However, playing at home could give Union a slight edge in a tightly contested match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between 1. FC Union Berlin and SC Freiburg ended in a goalless draw, with both teams failing to convert their opportunities. SC Freiburg dominated possession and created more dangerous chances, but poor finishing prevented them from taking the lead. Union Berlin, while less dominant in terms of ball control, showed resilience in defense and had moments of threat in the final third. Both sides have reasons to feel optimistic going into the second half, but Freiburg will likely feel they should be ahead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance
halftimeSections.text: SC Freiburg showcased their intent with a higher number of entries into the final third and penalty area, creating several big chances but failing to capitalize on them. Their aerial duels were less successful compared to Union Berlin, who won a significantly higher percentage of such battles, giving them an edge in set-piece situations. Union Berlin, despite lower possession, managed to hold their own defensively, with key clearances and tackles preventing Freiburg from breaking the deadlock. The home side needs to be more clinical in attack to turn their defensive solidity into points.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides, with Freiburg often dictating the pace in the early stages and sustaining several prolonged attacking spells. Union Berlin had their moments, particularly in short bursts around the middle of the half, pushing forward with intent. However, Freiburg regained control as the half progressed, mounting consistent pressure in the latter minutes. This ebb and flow suggests that while Freiburg has been more dominant in attack, Union Berlin remains dangerous during quick transitions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players
halftimeSections.text: For Union Berlin, Danilho Doekhi stood out defensively with strong tackling, while András Schäfer showed promise with a shot on target and an interception. On the Freiburg side, Vincenzo Grifo was a creative force in midfield, frequently involved in setting up plays, and Lucas Höler posed a threat with a shot on target despite missing a big chance. Both teams will look to these players to make a difference in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Given Freiburg's dominance in possession and chance creation, they are likely to continue pressing for a breakthrough in the second half. Their higher expected goals in the first half indicate they are closer to scoring, provided they improve their finishing. Union Berlin, on the other hand, will likely focus on counter-attacks and set-pieces, leveraging their aerial strength. Tactical adjustments from either manager could shift the balance, but Freiburg appears to have the upper hand if they maintain their intensity.
